Languas galanga, also known as Languas illustris, is a type of rhizome that belongs to the ginger family (Zingiberaceae) and is commonly used in traditional medicine. The rhizome of Languas galanga has been used for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, relieving pain and nausea, and aiding in digestion. The bioactive constituents of Languas galanga include gingerol, shogaol, and galangin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Languas galanga include teas, infusions, and powders, which can be used to relieve digestive issues and alleviate pain and inflammation.

This plant is native to tropical Asia, specifically in the regions of India, Sri Lanka, and Southeast Asia. Languas illustris is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the order Zingiberales. It is closely related to other plants in the Zingiberaceae family, such as Alpinia and Kaempferia.
